## [00:00 - 01:29]

When it comes to AI and especially for us and our team using it, one of the mindsets that we're constantly reminding ourselves is trust but verify. So in some situation like that where you are trusting that the algorithm is going to choose and pull the right kind of targeting for you. How could you verify that it's actually working the way that you need it to. It's kind of always been that way Facebook ads to right there are still weaknesses in the algorithm and optimization because essentially you tell meta that you have a certain goal and it's entire focus now is is giving you as many of those things as possible at the lowest price so that's not necessarily problematic if you say hey I want sales because if you can get you a bunch of sales but if you tell it. I want to get leads or engagement or link clicks or something else it could find weaknesses where like there's. Save bots or just people just click on everything and it can get you a whole bunch of those people think hey you should be happy you have all this stuff that you said that you wanted but you assumed that they would do other things that they never did so yeah it's always a matter of number one if something seems too good to be true in your results probably is definitely seen that but number two like. It's easiest to verify I think when. You're optimizing for some some type of conversion especially a purchase.
